title: HorizonMath — verifier-attackable target subset (staged offline)
note: HorizonMath is a benchmark of auto-verifiable open/research mathematics problems. This file stages the VERIFIER-ATTACKABLE subset: the problems that map to a frozen Vela verifier (a `vela campaign` kind) and carry a real incumbent (value-to-beat). It is NOT the full 101-problem corpus; the difference-triangle flagship is documented from the Constellate ingestion memo, the construction families from the live Open Constructions board (apps/web/app/constructions/page.tsx). Provenance is recorded per problem in `source`. Deterministic, offline: the adapter reads this file, mints content-addressed findings, attaches signed anchors.

corpus.staged_here: the verifier-attackable subset (problems with a Vela verifier_kind + a frozen checker); the remainder is deferred, not fabricated

problems.statement: Difference Triangle Set DTS(7,5): 7 rows, each a strictly increasing sequence of 6 marks starting at 0 (order 5), with every within-row pairwise difference globally distinct across all rows. Minimize the scope (the largest mark used).

incumbent.basis: HorizonMath value-to-beat for DTS(7,5) (Constellate memo §5.4); beating it needs a diverse solver portfolio (SAT/CP-SAT/MILP/local-search)

problems.statement: Cap set in F_3^8: maximize a subset of the affine space over GF(3) with no three points on a line (no three summing to zero).

incumbent.basis: DeepMind FunSearch (2023); the engine's greedy is far below this

problems.statement: Sidon set: a set of integers with all pairwise sums distinct; maximize the size within a given range (OEIS A309370).

incumbent.basis: OEIS A309370, Vela-improved lower bounds a(8..24); larger n open

problems.statement: Sidon set in GF(2)^n: a set of binary vectors with all pairwise XORs distinct; maximize the size (OEIS A394031).

incumbent.basis: OEIS A394031, listed through a(10)=34; the engine matches through a(9), falls short at a(10) (31 vs 34)

problems.statement: Covering design C(v,k,t): a family of k-blocks of [v] so every t-subset lies in at least one block; minimize the number of blocks.

incumbent.basis: La Jolla covering repository; many (v,k,t) entries open between best lower and upper bound

problems.statement: Constant-weight binary code A(n,d,w): binary codewords of length n, weight w, pairwise Hamming distance >= d; maximize the count.

incumbent.basis: Brouwer's tables; hundreds of A(n,d,w) entries have a lower/upper-bound gap

problems.statement: Costas array: a permutation matrix whose displacement vectors are all distinct; decide existence at every order.

incumbent.basis: known to exist at orders 1..29; orders 32 and 33 are open (no published array)

problems.statement: Union-free family: subsets of {1..n} where no member is the union of two others; maximize the count (OEIS A347025).

incumbent.basis: OEIS A347025, listed through a(7)=44; the engine misses a(6) by one (23 vs 24), then runs as a lower bound

problems.statement: Rook directions: place n non-attacking rooks to maximize the number of distinct pairwise directions (OEIS A321531).

incumbent.basis: OEIS A321531, exact maxima through a(10)=23; the engine reproduces every listed term, then extends as verified lower bounds a(11..20)

problems.statement: B_3 set in {0,1}^n: binary vectors with all 3-fold sums distinct (a Sidon-order-3 set); maximize the size.

incumbent.basis: exact maxima known for small n only; the engine holds verified lower bounds n=0..12 (best 46 at n=12)

problems.statement: Golomb ruler: integer marks with all pairwise differences distinct; find the shortest ruler of each order.

incumbent.basis: optimal lengths proven through order 28 (OGR project); a verified showcase, not an open frontier
